summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that selective list and selective extract crash with RangeError on a deeply nested tar entry while the unfiltered control paths complete safely.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should show plain list and plain extract completing successfully, while selective list and selective extract exit nonzero with RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded and stack frames in dist/esm/list.js.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit 1f0c2c9006b10199cf2686f8ef43e79a1773e1aa, built the target package, and verified that the plain list/extract controls exited 0 while selective list and selective extract both exited 1 with RangeError traces rooted in dist/esm/list.js:34.
verification_method: The verification helper spawns child Node processes so the crashing path cannot terminate the verifier itself. It asserts that unfiltered list/extract succeed, then checks that selective list/extract fail with RangeError and mapHas stack frames in dist/esm/list.js.
evidence_produced_by_run: The plain-list control process exited 0 and printed plain-list-ok.
The plain-extract control process exited 0 and printed TAR_ENTRY_ERROR:path excessively deep.
The selective-list and selective-extract child processes both exited 1 with RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded and stack traces through dist/esm/list.js:34.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
